parsed_review.summary: CP00-694 read-only review: PASS with one non-blocking P3. The pack is an honest descriptor-only RP23 finance-integrations foundation (150 units, risk C) that claims production_ready=false/runtime_ready=false pending this review; all distributions, validators, no-write/no-real-data boundaries, Claude read-only/non-final authority, and Hermes control-plane-only bounds verify cleanly, and review-hardening preconditions (valid auth preflight, allowed tools, prompt size, no invalid prior attempts) are met. The single P3 is the accepted live-pack-via-manifest-snapshot coverage topology shared with CP00-690→693, mitigated by frozen requirement constants and a matching ledger SHA.

findings.title: Live pack coverage resolved via manifest plan_binding_snapshot rather than an independent plan-array entry
findings.details: The live docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json carries CP00-694 only in live_state (lines 35-41); its packs[] array begins at CP00-695 (line 401). As a result both the model test (packages/finance-integrations/test/model.test.js:23-30, cp694PlanPack) and the contract-validation script (scripts/validate-rp23-external-integrations-ii-contract.mjs:30) resolve the CP00-694 plan pack through the `?? manifest?.plan_binding_snapshot` fallback, so the live pack's coverage is validated against a snapshot embedded in the pack's own manifest rather than an independent plan-array entry. Risk is low: validateFinanceIntegrationsCp694Coverage (validators.js:44-78) checks that snapshot against the frozen FINANCE_INTEGRATIONS_CP694_REQUIREMENTS constants hard-coded in registry.js:55-96 (deliverable/phase/micro-phase/micro-title counts, independently re-verified here to sum to 150), which serve as the code-reviewed anchor, and the manifest plan_binding_snapshot shares the live plan's source_ledger_sha (13c7ed65e83631946ea7fae75c4d53a6429b2b41576fe099027dfaffda91fa32; manifest line 4990 == closeout-pack-plan.json line 7 == next-pack-queue.json line 7), indicating common generator provenance. This is the same topology recorded as a non-blocking P3 in the CP00-690, CP00-691, CP00-692, and CP00-693 reviews.
findings.recommendation: No change required for closeout. Optionally, a future generator run could emit the current/live pack into plan.packs[] (or a dedicated closed-packs array) so the live pack's coverage is cross-validated against a plan-level entry independent of its own manifest.
findings.post_review_disposition: Accepted as expected, low-risk design; non-blocking. The frozen FINANCE_INTEGRATIONS_CP694_REQUIREMENTS constants in registry.js are the independent coverage anchor and the manifest snapshot shares the live plan's ledger SHA, so the verification chain remains sound.
